nodes.name: 霍克湾大区
nodes.summary: 霍克湾大区是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联1种鸟类，包括黑長腳鷸，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: 栖息地破坏
nodes.summary: 开发、采伐和围垦等活动导致适宜生境减少。

nodes.name: 黑長腳鷸
nodes.summary: 黑長腳鷸（Black stilt，学名 Himantopus novaezelandiae），分类上属于鸻形目，并归入反嘴鹬科，在图谱中标记为极危（CR）物种，已关联7个分布地点节点，常见栖息地包括湿地，主要威胁包括栖息地破坏。

nodes.name: Kaipara Harbour
nodes.summary: Kaipara Harbour是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联1种鸟类，包括黑長腳鷸，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: 反嘴鹬科
nodes.summary: 反嘴鹬科（Recurvirostridae）是图谱中的科级分类节点，当前关联6种鸟类，如斑长脚鹬、美洲反嘴鹬、Andean avocet等6种鸟类。

nodes.name: 菲奥德兰
nodes.summary: 菲奥德兰是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联4种鸟类，包括黑長腳鷸、峽灣企鵝、Grey-backed storm petrel等4种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: 鸻形目
nodes.summary: 鸻形目（Charadriiformes）是图谱中的目级分类节点，当前关联310种鸟类，如勺嘴鹬、短腿鸻、白鞘嘴鸥等310种鸟类。

nodes.name: CR
nodes.summary: CR表示 IUCN 极危等级，当前关联174种鸟类，如勺嘴鹬、加州神鹫、菲律宾鹰等174种鸟类。

nodes.name: Kawhia Harbour
nodes.summary: Kawhia Harbour是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联1种鸟类，包括黑長腳鷸，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: 约翰·古尔德
nodes.summary: 约翰·古尔德是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联71种鸟类，包括绿棉凫、凍原豆雁、黄腹角雉等71种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: 坎特伯雷大区
nodes.summary: 坎特伯雷大区是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联2种鸟类，包括灰鸭、黑長腳鷸，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: 辮狀河流
nodes.summary: 辮狀河流是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联1种鸟类，包括黑長腳鷸，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: 湿地
nodes.summary: 浅水、沼泽与植被斑块组成的高生产力生态系统。

links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: The black stilt or kakī (Māori), (Himantopus novaezelandiae) is a wading bird found in New Zealand

links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: Although black stilts currently breed only in braided river systems in the South Island, subfossil bones show that prior to human arrival they were found in habitats as diverse as narrow bush streams in Hawkes Bay, and a wetland lake surrounded by forest in North Canterbury. In the nineteenth century they bred on riverbeds and in wetlands of the central and eastern North Island and most of the South Island except Fiordland

links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: Although black stilts currently breed only in braided river systems in the South Island, subfossil bones show that prior to human arrival they were found in habitats as diverse as narrow bush streams in Hawkes Bay, and a wetland lake surrounded by forest in North Canterbury. In the nineteenth century they bred on riverbeds and in wetlands of the central and eastern North Island and most of the South Island except Fiordland

links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: Although black stilts currently breed only in braided river systems in the South Island, subfossil bones show that prior to human arrival they were found in habitats as diverse as narrow bush streams in Hawkes Bay, and a wetland lake surrounded by forest in North Canterbury. In the nineteenth century they bred on riverbeds and in wetlands of the central and eastern North Island and most of the South Island except Fiordland

links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: Although black stilts currently breed only in braided river systems in the South Island, subfossil bones show that prior to human arrival they were found in habitats as diverse as narrow bush streams in Hawkes Bay, and a wetland lake surrounded by forest in North Canterbury. In the nineteenth century they bred on riverbeds and in wetlands of the central and eastern North Island and most of the South Island except Fiordland

links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: Most black stilts will also overwinter in the Mackenzie Basin, but about 10% of the population, especially hybrids and those paired with pied stilts, migrate to North Island harbours such as Kawhia and Kaipara in January for the winter

links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: Most black stilts will also overwinter in the Mackenzie Basin, but about 10% of the population, especially hybrids and those paired with pied stilts, migrate to North Island harbours such as Kawhia and Kaipara in January for the winter

links.label: 栖息于
links.evidence: Although black stilts currently breed only in braided river systems in the South Island, subfossil bones show that prior to human arrival they were found in habitats as diverse as narrow bush streams in Hawkes Bay, and a wetland lake surrounded by forest in North Canterbury. In the nineteenth century they bred on riverbeds and in wetlands of the central and eastern North Island and most of the South Island except Fiordland

links.label: 受威胁于
links.evidence: Black stilts largely breed in the Mackenzie Basin in the South Island, and are threatened by introduced feral cats, ferrets, and hedgehogs as well as habitat degradation from hydroelectric dams, agriculture, and invasive weeds
